_comment: Pinned config for all LLM grader evaluations. Weights match GRADING_RUBRICS.md; re-graded sessions must use the same model_version + weights to be comparable.

correctness.description: Passes hidden tests, fixes planted traps

idiomatic.description: Proper language conventions, standard library use

no_ai_defects.description: No new races, security holes, hallucinated APIs, or unnecessary abstractions from AI

context_framing.description: Provides file contents, errors, constraints upfront

decomposition.description: Breaks complex tasks into 2-3 focused prompts

iterative_refinement.description: Specific feedback on wrong output ('line 12 has a race because...')

debug_loop.description: After test failure, includes failing assertion + relevant snippet

prompt_classification_ladder.5: Model-native: minimal-but-sufficient context, structured prompts, 1-2 tries
prompt_classification_ladder.4: Professional: cites exact errors, types, line numbers; 2-3 iterations max
prompt_classification_ladder.3: Competent: clear problems, some context, occasional back-and-forth
prompt_classification_ladder.2: Naive: generic descriptions, over-relies on model to figure out intent
prompt_classification_ladder.1: Counterproductive: massive dumps, 'fix this', no context

why_before_how.description: Asks 'what are the tradeoffs of X vs Y' before 'implement Z'

algorithm_choice.description: Only credited if candidate selected the algorithm (not starter)

data_structure_choice.description: Only credited if candidate selected the structure (not starter)

concurrency_design.description: Lock placement, primitive choice, deadlock avoidance

edge_case_awareness.description: Boundary handling, capacity constraints, unexpected inputs

constraint_driven.description: Solution respects stated constraints because candidate specified them

not_over_engineered.description: Didn't add AI-suggested abstractions the problem doesn't need
